questions.question: What is the primary purpose of using the Self-Organizing Map (SOM) algorithm for unsupervised learning?
options: A) To classify data into predefined categories
B) To identify patterns and structures in the data
C) To train a supervised model
D) To compress data
questions.explanation: The SOM algorithm is used to identify patterns and structures in the data by visualizing high-dimensional data in a lower-dimensional space.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key characteristic of the SOM algorithm?
options: A) It uses a supervised learning approach
B) It is a type of clustering algorithm
C) It is a type of dimensionality reduction technique
D) It is a type of neural network
questions.explanation: The SOM algorithm is a type of clustering algorithm that maps high-dimensional data to a lower-dimensional space.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of using the SOM algorithm for unsupervised learning?
options: A) It is faster than other clustering algorithms
B) It is more accurate than other clustering algorithms
C) It can handle high-dimensional data
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: The SOM algorithm can handle high-dimensional data and is known for its ability to identify complex patterns and structures in the data.

questions.question: Which of the following is a common application of the SOM algorithm?
options: A) Image classification
B) Text classification
C) Anomaly detection
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: The SOM algorithm has been successfully applied to various domains, including image and text classification, as well as anomaly detection.

questions.question: What is the primary difference between the SOM algorithm and other clustering algorithms?
options: A) It uses a supervised learning approach
B) It is a type of dimensionality reduction technique
C) It uses a different algorithm to identify clusters
D) It is a type of neural network
questions.explanation: The SOM algorithm uses a different algorithm to identify clusters, known as the competitive learning algorithm, which is distinct from other clustering algorithms such as k-means.
